symptom: Tests written for JWT-based refresh verification, but implementation uses opaque UUID tokens stored in DB. jwt.verify mock has no effect — service proceeds to DB lookup and returns access token instead of throwing.
fix_pattern: When refresh tokens are opaque UUIDs (DB-stored), tests must mock DB state (findUnique) to trigger rejection guards. JWT mock setup is irrelevant — remove it. Ensure inactive user + expired token + not found cases are covered via DB mock.
